summary.intent: Implement GridList adapter components for Leptos and Dioxus (tasks #398 and #490), then iteratively improve with better UX parity against React Aria, drag-and-drop features, richer examples across all widget variants, restructured implementation docs, and an agnostic-first architecture.
summary.outcome: GridList adapters are fully implemented with DnD, section rendering, load-more, selection modes, and parity features; a documentation-only PR was pushed splitting adapter delivery docs into focused workflow files; the full GridList implementation remains uncommitted pending CI and review.

repo: Adapter test files must be named `test_*.rs` (not `component.rs`) to be discovered by `cargo xtask lint adapter-parity`.
The post-implementation-audit skill at `.agents/skills/post-implementation-audit/SKILL.md` is mandatory after any adapter component and must be invoked before committing.
Dioxus `EventHandler<T>` is not `Send + Sync`, so it cannot be captured in agnostic `Callback` props; adapters must bridge callbacks after core state updates instead.
Leptos `view!` macro does not accept turbofish syntax inside prop expressions; move typed collections into a helper function or local binding first.

`cargo xtask spec validate` enforces anatomy part lists against conformance tests in `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/`; adding new Part variants requires updating both the spec and the conformance expected list.
E2E fixture categories must use `categories/<category>/<component>.rs` module structure; appending hundreds of lines to a flat category file is a workflow violation.

Native Dioxus web events are accessible via `dioxus_web::WebEventExt` (available once `dioxus-web` is added as an optional `web`-feature dependency) to call browser APIs like `DataTransfer.set_drag_image`.
The agnostic GridList machine already exposes `api.render_blocks()`, `api.drop_target()`, and `api.drag_keys()` — adapters should consume these rather than recomputing DnD state locally.
React Aria / React Spectrum is the mandatory primary counterpart for adapter component parity review; Ark UI is second, Radix UI third.

Widget examples must not reimplement component behavior (drag-key resolution, drop-target computation, reorder math); they should only hold demo item state and apply emitted `CollectionDndEvent::Reorder` callbacks.

code.finding: Core owns all GridList behavior: render blocks, grid preview ordering, DnD drag-key resolution, drop validity, disabled behavior, press-up selection, load-more suppression, and hover/press/drop-target state attrs.
Adapter consumes `api.render_blocks()` and `ItemRenderState`; drag image setup via `DataTransfer` is gated under `#[cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]` to avoid missing `web-sys` methods in native builds.
Drop handler uses `api.drop_target().cloned()` as primary target, falling back to event-cell hit-testing; this fixes grid-preview drops snapping back when the dragged card moves under the pointer.
Synthetic drag helpers must reacquire the DOM target after `dragstart` triggers a Dioxus/Leptos re-render; stale references cause dragover/drop to fire but produce no state change.

Added mandatory rule: fully read `docs/implementation/adapter-component-delivery.md` and linked workflow files before planning/implementing any component in `crates/ars-leptos` or `crates/ars-dioxus`; checklist is not a substitute.

Documented the agnostic-first boundary: shared behavior (selection, DnD, load-more, sections, hover/press state) belongs in `crates/ars-components`; adapters own only DOM refs, framework event translation, browser APIs, and rendering.

workflow: Run `cargo xtask spec component-deps` and `cargo xtask spec issue-deps --dry-run` before any adapter implementation to verify blockers are clear.
TDD order for adapter work: add failing agnostic core tests first, green them, then add adapter SSR tests, then wasm tests, then E2E fixtures, then examples.
Browser-based counterpart UX review (live React Aria docs page) must happen before planning adapter components, not as a late review step.
When moving persisted reorder into `on_dnd_event`, also set `dnd_enabled=true` on the GridList component — without it the machine rejects all DnD events and items snap back on drop.
`cargo +nightly fmt` must be applied before the final checks; snapshot files should be accepted with `INSTA_UPDATE=always cargo test` rather than manual copy.

Splitting a large flat Rust module into a directory-backed module (`foo.rs` → `foo/mod.rs`) requires updating all parent `mod.rs` and `main.rs` registrations, but does not require touching `Cargo.toml`.
The in-app browser tool cannot reliably dispatch native HTML drag events via script evaluation; rely on the `cargo xtask e2e` Playwright/WebDriver path for drag-and-drop coverage instead.

friction: Sandbox blocked binding to localhost for `dx serve` and fixture dev servers on multiple occasions, requiring repeated escalation to run E2E and wasm tests.
The in-app browser could not dispatch `DragEvent`, `MouseEvent`, or invoke `new DataTransfer()` from script evaluation, making interactive drag verification impossible through that path.
`dx serve` frequently got stuck showing a rebuild overlay in the in-app browser tab even after successful hot-reload, making visual confirmation ambiguous.

Multiple DnD snap-back bugs required several debug-and-fix cycles: always-before insertion, core not receiving `DragStart` because `dnd_enabled` was not passed, grid preview moving under the pointer so `drop` never fired, and stale element references in synthetic E2E drag helpers.
Dioxus reactive rendering did not invalidate on core DnD state changes until the render memo explicitly included `api.drop_target()` and `api.drag_keys()` as inputs.
Leptos `view!` macro turbofish restriction forced moving collection constructions into helper functions in multiple places.

The `cargo xtask spec validate` spec-conformance test has a hardcoded expected part list in `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/` that must be manually kept in sync with spec and `Part::all()` changes.

Dioxus `EventHandler` thread-safety constraint required a non-obvious workaround: use a pure no-op `Callback` in core props to mark load-more presence, then bridge the real `EventHandler` in the adapter after core events fire.
open_items: Checkbox adapter components (Leptos and Dioxus) need to be implemented in a separate session before revisiting GridList selection indicator reuse.
Full GridList React Aria parity gaps remain: keyboard DnD, `shouldSelectOnPressUp` in browser tests, full `IntersectionObserver`-based load-more triggering, and cross-collection drop support.
The GridList implementation branch (`codex/issues-398-490-gridlist-adapters`) has not been committed, pushed, or PR'd yet; `cargo xci-fast` and user approval are required before that step.

Utility E2E fixture (`crates/ars-e2e/fixtures/*/src/categories/utility/showcase.rs`) is still a combined single-file showcase; should be split per-component when utility primitives are individually implemented.
The E2E debug dump helpers added during drag-and-drop debugging are still present in `crates/ars-e2e/src/data_display/grid_list.rs` and should be cleaned up before final PR.

The spec overstatement in the agnostic `grid-list.md` parity section ('full parity / gaps none') was corrected but the bounded N/A matrix listing all specific React Aria gaps was only partially filled in.
The `waiting-for-codex-review` skill loop after PR creation was not yet invoked since no GridList implementation PR exists yet.
